issues: Coach's name is wrong: the OCR reads 'Rich Kurtenbach' which is clearly a truncation/OCR error of 'Orland Kurtenbach' (his first name does not appear in the OCR, but 'Rich' is not his name — the archive key fact is his name is Orland Kurtenbach; 'Rich' appears to be OCR noise from 'Richmond Kurtenbach' being garbled). The draft uses 'Rich Kurtenbach' which is incorrect.

The draft states the coach said the team 'lacked intensity' — the actual quote from the OCR is 'We got outworked, outplayed, out-everythinged' and 'The oomph just wasn't there', which is a different characterization than 'lacked intensity'.

The headline in the draft ('Humboldt dominates Richmond in Centennial Cup round robin') is not confirmed by the OCR — no clear headline is visible in the OCR text provided beyond the dateline 'HUMBOLDT'.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Humboldt Broncos defeat Richmond Sockeyes 6-1 in Centennial Cup round-robin play, with goals from Stan Czenczek and Steve Jaques involved in key plays.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Humboldt Broncos dominated the Richmond Sockeyes 6-1 in Centennial Cup round-robin play on May 7, 1987, in what turned into one of the most lopsided results in eight games between the two Western finalists. Stan Czenczek tied the game 1-1 in the first period with a hard slapshot, but Humboldt's Garnet Kazuik scored a shorthanded breakaway goal in the second period after stealing the puck from Steve Jaques, opening the floodgates. Coach Orland Kurtenbach lamented "We got outworked, outplayed, out-everythinged — the 'oomph' just wasn't there." Richmond finished the round robin 2-1 and advanced to a semi-final against Pembroke, with the Broncos (3-0) going directly to the championship game.
review.notes: The OCR renders the coach's name as "Rich Kurtenbach" — this is almost certainly an OCR/truncation artifact of "Richmond" running into "Kurtenbach" in the text (i.e., "said Richmond coach Kurtenbach" garbled). The known head coach is Orland Kurtenbach; corrected accordingly. No clear standalone headline is visible in the OCR. Byline is absent.
